prompt: Design a Local Travel & Experience Booking Portal to let customers search, book, and manage offline travel services while sellers (photographers and other providers) fulfill orders and administrators govern content and compliance. The English UI, built with jQuery for responsive forms and live validation, starts with a username/password sign-in and role-based landing pages for Customer, Photographer (Seller), and Administrator. Customers can browse using keyword search with hierarchical destination filters (e.g., State → City → Neighborhood) plus date, price range, rating, theme, and transport mode filters, with sortable results and pagination; the interface also surfaces query suggestions from locally stored popular terms. Product pages show available time slots, estimated delivery or pickup ETA for physical items (e.g., printed photos), and clear order status feedback. Customers create orders, confirm details, select pickup or courier delivery, and manage shipping addresses with create/edit/delete, a single default address, and basic validation for US ZIP code and state consistency. An in-app messaging center supports real-time buyer–seller chat for coordination, including image attachments (JPEG/PNG up to 5 MB) and visible read/unread state. System notifications appear in-app for holds, due and overdue reminders, approval results, and order status changes; users can set preferences to mute non-critical reminders while keeping mandatory compliance notices. The backend uses Spring MVC to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by the jQuery frontend, and MyBatis with MySQL for durable local persistence, ensuring the entire system runs without internet connectivity. Access control enforces role permissions and fine-grained resource rules, such as restricting photographers to their own listings, chats, and orders; administrators can blacklist accounts for a configurable duration (default 7 days) with an auditable reason and automatic re-enable at expiry. Order workflow is modeled as strict state transitions (create → confirm → pay recorded → check-in verified → check-out → complete, with cancel/reschedule/refund branches) and guarded by idempotency tokens to prevent duplicate submissions for 10 minutes per order action. Unpaid orders auto-close after 30 minutes, releasing inventory; oversell prevention uses transactional row locking on time-slot availability and compensating rollback if downstream steps fail. Notifications are stored locally with template rendering, retry up to 3 times on failure, and read/archive tracking; any optional email/SMS outputs are limited to locally queued “to-send” records for later manual export, never external delivery. Points and awards are calculated by configurable rules across individual, class, department, and team scopes, with tie-breakers prioritized by completion count then earliest completion timestamp; manual adjustments require admin role, a mandatory note, and immutable audit logs. Sensitive data such as passwords and phone numbers (if stored for contact only) must be hashed or encrypted at rest, and masked in UI views and logs to minimize exposure.
